description: Phase 1 tasks for BoringOS v1. Each task is a self-contained Claude Code session (~1-3 hours). Sequence per docs/phases/phase-1.md.

tasks.name: Create @boringos/app-sdk package skeleton
tasks.goal: Bootstrap the public SDK package so subsequent tasks have somewhere to land code.
acceptance: Package compiles via `pnpm -F @boringos/app-sdk build`
License file is MIT (matches package.json license field)
Empty index.ts exports placeholder (no errors)
pnpm-workspace.yaml already includes @boringos/* (verify)
doNot: Add functional types yet — that is B2
Publish to npm — that is B5

tasks.name: Define ConnectorManifest and AppManifest types
tasks.goal: Codify the manifest contract per Phase 0 naming decisions (kind, id, type, name).
acceptance: Exports `Manifest`, `ConnectorManifest`, `AppManifest`, `PublisherInfo`, `EntityTypeDeclaration`, `UIManifest`
Field naming matches Phase 0: extension uses `kind`, instance uses `id`, events use `type`, actions use `name`
AppManifest includes hosting field as `hosting: "in-process"` (no remote in v1)
Capabilities declared as string array; full catalog cross-referenced to capabilities.md
doNot: Validate manifests at runtime yet — that is C4
Define helper functions (defineApp etc.) — that is B3

tasks.name: Implement defineApp, defineConnector, defineUI helpers
tasks.goal: Provide ergonomic builders that produce typed AppDefinition / ConnectorDefinition / UIDefinition values.
acceptance: `defineApp({...})` returns typed AppDefinition
`defineConnector({...})` returns typed ConnectorDefinition (matches existing @boringos/connector shape)
`defineUI({...})` returns typed UIDefinition with slot contributions
Type errors when required fields missing
doNot: Implement runtime that consumes these — that's the install pipeline (C5)

tasks.name: Define slot types and lifecycle context types
tasks.goal: Surface the slot APIs and runtime contexts apps will use inside their handlers.
acceptance: Exports: NavSlot, DashboardWidget, EntityAction, EntityDetailPanel, SettingsPanel, CommandAction, CopilotTool, InboxHandler
InboxHandler typed as UI-only (no agent wake fields)
Exports: LifecycleContext, ContextBuildContext, ActionContext, ToolContext
All types compile cleanly
doNot: Implement slot rendering in shell — that is A6
Implement context building runtime — separate Phase 2 work

tasks.name: Publish @boringos/app-sdk@1.0.0-alpha.0 + @boringos/connector-sdk@1.0.0-alpha.0
tasks.goal: Make the SDK installable from npm under the @boringos scope.
acceptance: Both packages published to npm at 1.0.0-alpha.0
@boringos/connector-sdk re-exports from @boringos/app-sdk (or contains connector-only types)
Tagged as alpha (npm dist-tag: alpha)
doNot: Bump to stable 1.0.0 — that happens after Phase 2 CRM port validates the contract
tasks.notes: Code prep in 572db21; npm publish executed in same session

tasks.name: Define boringos.json manifest JSON Schema
tasks.goal: Provide a machine-readable schema validating connector and app manifests.
acceptance: JSON Schema validates the same shapes the TypeScript types describe
validate() function returns structured errors
Tests cover both connector and app manifest variants
doNot: Validate against real connectors yet — that is D2

tasks.name: Migrate @boringos/connector-slack to manifest format
tasks.goal: Add boringos.json + JSON Schema action I/O + capability declarations to Slack connector. **This is the contract validation gate.**
acceptance: boringos.json present at package root
Manifest validates against D1 schema
Capabilities declared: auth:oauth:slack, events:emit:slack.*, actions:expose:3, webhooks:receive:/events, network:outbound:slack.com
Existing TS factory function unchanged (must be backwards compatible)
Slack connector still works in existing tests
doNot: Touch the runtime behavior
Migrate Google yet (D3) — surface format issues first
tasks.criticalGate: If this task is hard or the SDK has to change to make it work, fix B before continuing. Do not merge a hacky D2.
tasks.notes: Gate green. One small SDK fix surfaced ($schema property in BaseFields).

tasks.name: Migrate @boringos/connector-google (Gmail + Calendar combined)
tasks.goal: Same migration as D2 but for the larger combined Google connector.
acceptance: Manifest covers both Gmail and Calendar event/action surfaces
Two outbound network domains declared: googleapis.com, accounts.google.com
All actions migrated to JSON Schema
Skill files referenced via skills field in manifest
doNot: Split into two packages (connector-gmail + connector-calendar) — keep combined for now
tasks.notes: Added skills field to manifest schema. Surfaced + fixed a stale tsbuildinfo issue.

tasks.name: CI verification job for connector manifests
tasks.goal: Make CI fail when a connector's manifest claims diverge from its code.
acceptance: Job runs on every PR touching packages/@boringos/connector-*/
Verifies: manifest validity (D1 schema), event types match TS, action count matches, OAuth scopes consistent, outbound network honesty
Fails build with actionable error messages
Slack and Google connectors pass
doNot: Run on app packages yet — that comes later
tasks.notes: Wrote scripts/verify-connectors.mjs (not the .ts originally specified). Negative-tested with corrupted manifest.

tasks.name: Create @boringos/shell package skeleton
tasks.goal: Bootstrap the shell SPA package so screens can be moved into it.
acceptance: `pnpm -F @boringos/shell dev` boots a blank React app
License is BSL 1.1
Depends on @boringos/ui and @boringos/app-sdk
doNot: Move CRM screens yet — those come in A3-A5

tasks.name: Define slot type contracts in the shell
tasks.goal: Implement the slot-rendering side of the slot types defined in B4 — the shell's runtime contract for app contributions.
acceptance: Slot registry tracks which apps contributed to which slots
SlotRenderer renders all contributions for a given slot id
Type-safe: rendering an EntityAction slot only accepts EntityAction contributions
doNot: Wire to real install pipeline yet — that is A6
tasks.notes: 11 unit tests for the registry; React layer (Provider/hooks/SlotRenderer) tested end-to-end starting at A5.

tasks.name: Move Layout, Sidebar, CommandBar from CRM web to shell
tasks.goal: Lift the shell chrome into @boringos/shell. Components are CRM-shaped today; generalize as part of the move.
acceptance: Files copied (not deleted from CRM yet — A8 strips CRM)
CRM-specific nav entries removed; sidebar reads nav contributions from slot registry
CommandBar reads command actions from slot registry
Compiles with no errors when used by @boringos/shell App.tsx
doNot: Delete from CRM — A8 does that
Hard-code any nav entries — they all come from slot contributions

tasks.name: Move auth screens (Login, Signup) from CRM to shell
tasks.goal: Authentication UI lives in the shell, not in apps.
acceptance: Login + Signup work against /api/auth/* endpoints (existing framework auth)
AuthProvider hooks usable by all shell screens
Signup with tenantName creates new tenant; signup with inviteCode joins existing
doNot: Build SSO yet (Phase 5)
tasks.notes: Renamed CRM's `orgName` → `tenantName` (correct framework field). Namespaced localStorage keys boringos.token / boringos.tenantId. User card + tenant switcher restored in Sidebar.

tasks.name: Move shared screens (Home, Copilot, Inbox, Tasks, Agents, Workflows, Settings)
tasks.goal: Lift the framework-level screens into the shell. Domain-specific screens stay in CRM (until Phase 2 strips them).
acceptance: Each screen functions on a fresh tenant with zero apps installed
Brief renamed to Home; layout adapted to read dashboard.widget contributions from slot registry
Copilot reads copilot.tool contributions from slot registry
Settings reads settings.panel contributions from slot registry
doNot: Move Pipeline, Contacts, Deals, Companies, KnowledgeBase — those are CRM-specific and stay in CRM
tasks.notes: First visual milestone. All 13 routes return 200; all 7 screen modules transform clean. Workflows uses direct admin API instead of @boringos/workflow-ui to keep shell light.

tasks.name: Implement slot registration runtime
tasks.goal: Wire the slot registry to the install pipeline so installed apps' slot contributions appear in the shell.
acceptance: When an app is installed, its UI definition's slot contributions are added to the registry
When uninstalled, removed
Slot renderer hot-updates without a page reload (event-driven)
doNot: Implement actual install pipeline — that is C5
Handle remote apps — v1 is in-process only
tasks.notes: InstallRuntime wraps SlotRegistry as the canonical hook C5 calls into. 10 tests cover install/reinstall/uninstall/server-only/list/clear/hot-update.

tasks.name: Build the Apps screen (Browse, Installed, Updates, Install from URL)
tasks.goal: The wp-admin Plugins page. The killer screen of v1.
acceptance: Browse tab shows curated marketplace listings (mock data acceptable in v1; backend deferred)
Installed tab lists tenant_apps with status and capabilities
Install from URL accepts a GitHub URL, fetches manifest, shows permission prompt
Permission prompt renders manifest capabilities grouped by category
doNot: Build the actual install transaction — that is C5
Build a marketplace backend — Phase 4
tasks.notes: C7 not done yet — A7 inlines a minimal PermissionPrompt at screens/Apps/PermissionPrompt.tsx that C7 will replace with the canonical shared component. Browse uses MOCK_LISTINGS until Phase 4 marketplace backend lands.

tasks.name: Strip CRM web of all moved code
tasks.goal: One PR in boringos-crm that removes the chrome and shared screens that now live in @boringos/shell.
acceptance: boringos-crm/packages/web no longer renders shell chrome
Domain components (DealForm, DossierView, ContactForm etc.) survive
CRM still builds (will not run as a standalone SPA — that is by design; full Phase 2 port restores it as an installable app)
doNot: Touch CRM server — that is Phase 2 work
tasks.completedCommit: fe2e35f (in boringos-crm, on branch feat_business_shell — not pushed)
tasks.notes: 12 lifted files deleted, App.tsx rewritten to keep CRM-specific routes only. Pre-existing breakage in CRM (stale link path to @boringos/workflow-ui from the framework rename) is out of A8's scope; A8 introduces zero new errors and removes one. Commit lives on feat_business_shell branch in CRM repo, not pushed — awaiting user OK before merging to CRM main.

tasks.name: Build BrandProvider + useBrand hook + Settings → Branding panel
tasks.goal: Make every visual element of the shell tenant-configurable. White-label by default; defaults to BoringOS branding.
acceptance: BrandProvider reads tenant_settings (brand.* keys) on auth, provides via React context
useBrand() returns: productName, productTagline, logoUrl, faviconUrl, primaryColor, secondaryColor, loginBackground, emailFromName
All shell chrome (Sidebar, Layout, Login, Signup, outbound emails) uses useBrand() — no hardcoded 'BoringOS' or logo paths anywhere
Defaults to BoringOS branding when tenant has not customized any brand.* setting
Settings → Branding panel: admin-only; logo upload via Drive; live color preview; reset-to-defaults button
useBrand exported from @boringos/app-sdk so apps can inherit tenant brand
Updating any brand setting reflects in the shell immediately (no full reload)
doNot: Implement custom subdomain routing — Enterprise tier (Phase 5)
Implement 'Hide Powered by BoringOS' enforcement — Enterprise tier (Phase 5)
Touch app code beyond exporting useBrand from the SDK — apps adopt it on their own schedule
tasks.notes: Brand type lives in @boringos/app-sdk; useBrand hook lives in @boringos/shell since it depends on a React context only the shell provides. BrandingPanel uses URL fields for logo/favicon/loginBackground; Drive-backed logo upload is a small follow-up once the Drive screen ships. Workstream A complete (9/9).

tasks.name: Migration: tenant_apps table
tasks.goal: Add the table that records which apps are installed in which tenants.
acceptance: Columns: id (uuid), tenant_id (uuid, FK), app_id (text), version (text), status (enum: active/paused/uninstalling), capabilities (jsonb), manifest_hash (text), installed_at (timestamp), updated_at (timestamp)
Index on (tenant_id, app_id)
Migration applies cleanly forward
doNot: Add cross-app links — that is C2

tasks.name: Migration: tenant_app_links table
tasks.goal: Record cross-app dependency approvals (app B reads app A's entities).
acceptance: Columns: id, tenant_id, source_app_id, target_app_id, capability (e.g. entities.crm:read), approved_at
Unique index on (tenant_id, source_app_id, target_app_id, capability)
doNot: Implement enforcement — that lives in the SDK runtime checks (separate work)

tasks.name: Manifest fetcher (GitHub URL → boringos.json + bundle)
tasks.goal: Resolve a GitHub URL to a fetchable, validatable manifest + bundle artifact.
acceptance: Accepts: github.com/org/repo, github.com/org/repo@vX.Y.Z, github.com/org/repo#branch
Resolves to latest tagged release if no version specified
Returns: { manifest, bundleUrl, hash }
License is BSL 1.1
doNot: Validate the manifest — that is C4
Run the install — that is C5
tasks.notes: Plus 15 fetcher tests with mocked fetch (URL parsing, latest-tag resolution, branch ref, version normalization, hashing, error paths).

tasks.name: Manifest validator (schema + capability honesty)
tasks.goal: Validate fetched manifests before any install begins.
acceptance: Validates against D1 schema
Enforces capability honesty: declared capabilities must match what the bundle actually does (parse bundle for SDK calls)
Returns structured errors with actionable messages
doNot: Run the install — that is C5
tasks.notes: Honesty layer is greppable rules in v1 (5 patterns; warnings, not errors). Strict AST-level analysis lands in Phase 4 marketplace pipeline. 12 tests covering both schema and honesty layers.

tasks.name: Install pipeline (atomic transaction)
tasks.goal: Take a validated manifest + bundle and install the app fully or roll back on any failure.
acceptance: Steps run inside a DB transaction: create tenant_apps row, run schema migrations, register agents, register workflow templates, mount routes, register slot contributions, run onTenantCreated
Any failure rolls back the entire transaction
Emits app.installed event on success
Updates slot registry (A6) so UI updates immediately
doNot: Implement remote-app dispatch — v1 is in-process only
Handle billing — Phase 5
tasks.notes: Pure function with injected db / slotRuntime / events. 10 tests covering happy paths, atomic rollback (slot-fail rolls back DB), idempotency (re-install uninstalls prior + reinstalls), and the don't-rollback-on-event-fail rule. TODO markers in source for kernel wiring (schema migrations, agent registration, route mounting, onTenantCreated) — C5 is the orchestrator, kernel adapter is its own task.

tasks.name: Uninstall pipeline (soft + hard)
tasks.goal: Reverse an install cleanly. Two modes: soft (retain data 30d), hard (drop everything).
acceptance: Soft: routes unmount, agents pause, slots unregister, tenant_apps marked uninstalling, data retained 30 days
Hard: soft steps + immediate drop of namespaced tables + delete files + remove agents
Cascade: if uninstalling app A and another app B has tenant_app_links to A, surface warning before proceeding
Emits app.uninstalled event
doNot: Implement automatic retention sweep cron — separate work
tasks.notes: Soft and hard modes; cascade returns dependent-app list (caller decides whether to force); event-emit failure doesn't break uninstall. 7 tests.

tasks.name: Permission prompt component
tasks.goal: OAuth-style consent UI shown before any install completes.
acceptance: Renders manifest capabilities grouped by category (Data, Agents & Workflows, UI, Integrations, Memory)
Shows verified-publisher badge when applicable
Shows 'Unverified publisher' warning for GitHub-direct installs
Approve button triggers C5 install pipeline; Cancel aborts cleanly
doNot: Allow individual capability dropping in v1 — all-or-nothing approval
tasks.notes: Canonical at components/PermissionPrompt.tsx; A7's local screens/Apps/PermissionPrompt.tsx now re-exports. Workstream C complete (7/7).

tasks.name: Build apps/generic-triage using @boringos/app-sdk
tasks.goal: First-party default app that classifies inbox items.
acceptance: Built using only the public @boringos/app-sdk (no shell internals)
Subscribes to inbox.item_created via workflow
Classifies items, scores importance, attaches metadata to inbox item
Does NOT draft replies (that is generic-replier's job)
Does NOT create entities (that is what apps like CRM do)
doNot: Hardcode CRM-specific logic — this is the generic version

tasks.name: Build apps/generic-replier using @boringos/app-sdk
tasks.goal: First-party default app that drafts a generic reply suggestion.
acceptance: Built using only the public @boringos/app-sdk
Subscribes to inbox.item_created
Drafts a generic reply suggestion and appends to the inbox item's suggestions list
Multiple suggestions coexist (does not overwrite suggestions from other apps)
doNot: Take ownership of an item — coexists with domain-specific repliers

tasks.name: Pre-install Generic Triage and Generic Replier at tenant provision
tasks.goal: A fresh tenant has both default apps installed automatically.
acceptance: On signup with tenantName, both apps are pre-installed via the regular install pipeline (C5)
Tenant can disable or uninstall either app — they follow the regular lifecycle
If pre-install fails, signup still succeeds; tenant is told one or both default apps could not be installed
doNot: Mark default apps as undeletable — they are uninstallable like any other app

tasks.name: Migrate CRM Email Triage logic into Generic Triage where appropriate
tasks.goal: Apply the layered split: generic classification stays in shell-default app; CRM-specific interpretation stays in CRM (Phase 2).
touch: (Phase 2 will handle the corresponding CRM side)
acceptance: Generic Triage handles: classification (lead/reply/internal/newsletter/spam), importance scoring, metadata attachment
Generic Triage does NOT: link to CRM Contacts, create Deals, draft CRM-aware replies (those are CRM's responsibilities, addressed in Phase 2)
Documented split is consistent with coordination.md
doNot: Touch CRM server code — that is Phase 2
